    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "3M has a long-standing reputation as a company committed to innovation. We provide the freedom to explore and encourage curiosity and creativity. We gain new insight from diverse thinking, and take risks on new ideas. Here, you can apply your talent in bold ways that matter. About 3M and the Consumer Business Group At 3M, we apply science in collaborative ways to improve everyday life. The Consumer Business Group (CBG) brings 3M’s trusted and innovative brands—such as Scotch®, Post-it®, Scotch-Brite®, and Command™—to homes and workplaces across India. We combine global technology leadership with deep local insights to deliver convenience, efficiency, and quality that customers trust. Role Overview The Regional Sales Manager (RSM) – CBG will lead the sales, distribution, and retail execution strategy for the assigned region. This role is responsible for driving business growth through effective distributor management, channel expansion, and frontline team leadership. The position demands a balance of strategic planning, operational excellence, and people management to deliver sustainable and profitable growth in alignment with 3M’s values and business priorities. Key Responsibilities Sales & Distribution Excellence Implement, monitor, and continuously improve the region’s sales and distribution infrastructure, processes, and performance metrics. Develop and execute regional sales plans and programs to meet or exceed revenue and market share goals. Drive sell-out at retail through demand generation activities, visibility, merchandising, and in-store execution initiatives. Ensure product range availability, right product placement, and width of distribution across channels. Team Leadership Lead, coach, and develop a team of Territory/Area Sales Managers to achieve sales targets and operational KPIs. Build a performance-driven culture with strong focus on capability building and customer-centric execution. Channel & Distributor Management Appoint and manage distributors/super stockists ensuring right investment, infrastructure, and service levels. Monitor ROI, claims, and ensure timely financial settlements and adherence to 3M policies. Strengthen trade partnerships through consistent engagement and transparent communication. Business & Market Insights Analyze market trends, channel dynamics, and competitor activities to identify growth opportunities. Provide regular inputs to marketing and category teams on pricing, promotions, and product performance. Operational Governance & Reporting Ensure accurate and timely reporting on sales performance, forecasts, budgets, and trade spends. Manage regional budgets effectively and ensure adherence to compliance, ethical, and operational standards. Key Accountabilities Achieving sales revenue and profitability targets for the region. Driving execution excellence in distribution, visibility, and trade programs. Timely and accurate claim settlements and financial control. Building strong customer relationships and ensuring high satisfaction levels. Adherence to 3M’s business ethics, compliance, and operational governance. Qualifications & Experience MBA or equivalent postgraduate qualification. 10–15 years of relevant experience in sales, distribution, and channel management —preferably from FMCG, Consumer Durables, or allied industries. Strong business acumen with proven success in leading regional teams and managing large-scale distributor networks. Excellent communication, analytical, and stakeholder management skills. Prior experience or familiarity with the assigned region is preferred. Safety is a core value at 3M. All employees are expected to contribute to a strong Environmental Health and Safety (EHS) culture by following safety policies, identifying hazards, and engaging in continuous improvement."
    }
  ],

      "content": "3M has a long-standing reputation as a company committed to innovation. We provide the freedom to explore and encourage curiosity and creativity. Role Summary The Demand Planning & S&OP Analyst is responsible for executing the Sales & Operations Planning (S&OP) process for assigned divisions in India. This role ensures strong alignment between demand, supply, inventory, and business priorities to deliver high customer service while optimizing working capital. The position works closely with cross-functional teams across Sales, Marketing, Manufacturing, Procurement, and Customer Service, and partners with global supply chain teams to deploy best practices. Primary Responsibilities S&OP & Demand Planning Lead the end-to-end S&OP process, ensuring alignment between demand and supply plans for assigned divisions. Review statistical demand forecasts, analyze internal and external demand drivers, and continuously improve forecast accuracy. Collaborate with business leaders to incorporate qualitative inputs and finalize consensus demand forecasts. Track forecast accuracy and bias; analyze demand variances and drive corrective actions. Supply Planning & Capacity Management Develop and maintain short-, mid-, and long-term supply plans based on demand forecasts, production capacity, and inventory policies. Monitor capacity requirements, special orders, service levels, and inventory performance. Identify capacity constraints, escalate risks, and coordinate mitigation plans with manufacturing plants, contract manufacturers, and procurement teams. Ensure timely product availability while minimizing excess and obsolete inventory. Inventory & Portfolio Management Optimize planning parameters such as lot size, order quantity, replenishment frequency, and production cycles. Drive reduction of non-working and excess inventory through proactive demand corrections and liquidation initiatives in collaboration with Sales and Customer Service teams. Assess product lifecycle stages and support SKU portfolio optimization to improve supply chain efficiency and profitability. Execution & Customer Service Manage product flow to support timely and reliable customer fulfillment. Review customer backorders and coordinate with cross-functional teams to minimize service disruptions. Identify opportunities to improve customer service levels, reduce inventory investment, and enhance end-to-end supply chain efficiency. Data Governance & Continuous Improvement Ensure integrity and governance of demand planning data in line with 3M policies and global standards. Lead local process and capability improvements and support adoption of global best practices. Support productivity initiatives, system enhancements, ERP implementations, and supply chain transformation projects."

  "experience": "8-10 years in SAP BW on HANA, ABAP, Native HANA modeling, and SQL/SQL Script",
  "locations": ["IN", "BANGALORE"],
  "educational_qualifications": "Bachelor’s degree or higher",
  "required_skill_set": [
    "SAP BW on HANA",
    "ABAP",
    "Native HANA modeling",
    "SQL/SQL Script",
    "Data generation, transformation, and consumption",
    "ABAP objects development",
    "Calculation Views",
    "HANA push-down logic",
    "ETL processes",
    "Performance optimization",
    "SQL query optimization"
  ],
  "remote_type": "On-site",
  "posted_on": "Posted 30+ Days Ago",
  "job_id": "R01160770",
  "salary": "",
  "is_active": true,
  "first_seen": "2023-10-23",
  "last_seen": "2023-10-23",
  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "3M has a long-standing reputation as a company committed to innovation. We provide the freedom to explore and encourage curiosity and creativity. We gain new insight from diverse thinking, and take risks on new ideas. Here, you can apply your talent in bold ways that matter. Job Description: Job Title Senior Data Engineer-SAP BW on HANA, ABAP & HANA, SQL Script Collaborate with Innovative 3Mers Around the World Choosing where to start and grow your career has a major impact on your professional and personal life, so it’s equally important you know that the company that you choose to work at, and its leaders, will support and guide you. With a wide variety of people, global locations, technologies and products, 3M is a place where you can collaborate with other curious, creative 3Mers. The Impact You’ll Make in this Role As a Sr. Data Engineer, you will have the opportunity to design, develop, and deliver high-performance enterprise analytics and data warehousing solutions leveraging SAP BW on HANA, ABAP, Native HANA modeling, and advanced SQL/SQL Script, ensuring scalable, optimized, and business-driven reporting solutions. You are expected to be an SME and responsible for team decisions, provide solutions to problems for their immediate team and across multiple teams, ability to troubleshoot and resolve technical issues efficiently. As a Sr. Data Engineer, you will have the opportunity to tap into your curiosity and collaborate with some of the most innovative people around the world. Here, you will make an impact by: Designing and developing SAP BW on HANA solutions including multi-layered data models with an object like Composite Providers, Open ODS Views. Deep understanding of data generation, transformation, and consumption across the full SAP Order-to-Cash lifecycle, including key tables, document flows, and SD–FI dependencies. Ability to trace complete data lineage from source transactions to downstream analytics and reporting layers. Building Native HANA models using Calculation Views (Graphical & Scripted), Stored procedures, optimized for real-time reporting and data consumption. Strong ability to convert SAP OTC business rules (pricing, billing, revenue recognition, returns) into trusted analytical datasets. Developing and enhancing ABAP objects including Function Modules, BAPIs, Enhancements, User Exits, and BADIs to support BW and HANA integrations. Hands-on experience extracting and integrating OTC data from SAP ECC/S/4HANA into enterprise data platforms. Implementing HANA push-down logic using AMDP, SQL Script, CDS Views to significantly improve data processing and query performance. Designing and optimizing ETL processes using BW transformations, DTPs, Process Chains, and HANA-based data flows. Acting as a bridge between SAP functional teams and analytics users to deliver business-aligned insights and KPIs. Writing and optimizing complex SQL queries to handle large data volumes efficiently. Leading performance optimization initiatives including query runtime analysis, HANA plan visualization, table partitioning, indexing strategies, and elimination of redundant BW layers. Providing production support, root cause analysis, and continuous improvement for BW and HANA environments. Your Skills and Expertise To set you up for success in this role from day one, 3M requires (at a minimum) the following qualifications: Bachelor’s degree or higher (completed and verified prior to start) 8–10 years of hands-on experience in SAP BW on HANA, ABAP, Native HANA modeling, and advanced SQL/SQL Script. Deep understanding of data generation, transformation, and consumption across the full SAP Order-to-Cash lifecycle, including key tables, document flows, and SD–FI dependencies. Previous experience in Developing and enhancing ABAP objects including Function Modules, BAPIs, Enhancements, User Exits, and BADIs to support BW and HANA integrations. Expertise in Native HANA models using Calculation Views (Graphical & Scripted). Additional qualifications that could help you succeed even further in this role include: Excellent communication with business stakeholders to gather, clarify, and validate requirements, ensuring a shared understanding across technical and non-technical teams. Translating complex business needs into scalable SAP BW on HANA, HANA, and Snowflake technical architecture designs. To be acting as a bridge between business and technical teams, aligning data models, reporting needs, and performance expectations. Producing clear documentation and presentations that explain architectural decisions, data flows, and system capabilities. Location: 3M Bangalore Global Technical Center, Bengaluru, Hybrid, at least 2 days in a week."
    }
  ],
  "Scrap_json": {}
}

That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become."

  "required_skill_set": [

  "remote_type": "On-site",

  "is_active": true,
  "first_seen": "2024-04-06",
  "last_seen": "2024-04-07",
  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Program Manager to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Make your mark in Programs. With over 50 years in the Programs space, AIG Programs is an industry leader in the U.S. Program business. We focus on developing strategic solutions designed to make Program Administrators’ lives easier. By leveraging our breadth of offerings and dedicated Underwriting, Claims and Loss Control teams, we offer exceptional service and solutions. We serve niche markets on both an admitted and non-admitted basis, through market leading infrastructure and underwriting expertise. How you will create an impact: A Program Manager is highly motivated, results oriented problem solver who exemplifies a willingness to be flexible, learn on the job and maintain a “can do” attitude. Program Manager is responsible for the underwriting, marketing & portfolio management of each assigned Program. Developing and negotiating business plans for each assigned program, including plans for new business, renewal rate management, expected retentions to position the division to achieve profit objectives. Participating in Underwriting Quality Reviews; conduct additional file reviews as needed. Positioning program growth in jurisdictions that are deemed rate adequate and managing to the technically correct price. Coordinating and ‘chair’ Stewardship meetings for assigned Programs. Identifying and marketing new products to round out existing portfolios and incrementally enhance portfolio of products afforded to our clients. Monitoring renewals, rate targets vs. achieved, retentions, trends, lost business, etc. on a monthly basis to assure achievement of business plan and profitability targets. Understanding each assigned program: the industries and their exposures to loss, updating program ‘eligibility’ and coverages afforded as needed to minimize adverse exposures to unanticipated loss. Actively identify training opportunities at the PA level to enhance their LOB knowledge and successfully guide Program Administrators in their execution on risk selection, pricing, coverage terms and conditions in a manner that is aligned with authorities granted by AIG. Actively understanding and managing the authority delegated by the PA to the underwriters assigned to our program(s). Oversight and monitor PA ‘peers review’ process. Developing a strong working knowledge of AIG operating systems (rating and policy issuance) at the PA level. Understanding, managing and minimizing AAL for each program. Developing line of business expertise for assigned Program(s). Identifying trends in the program profiles, communicating & implementing appropriate measures by LOB in order to protect the portfolio and AIG from adverse exposures. Demonstrating an understanding of industry changes and potential impact by r LOB through clearly articulated recommendations for actions during the account referral process. Ensuring Program Administrator is meeting AIG’s underwriting expectations and is aligned with program business plans through the execution and management of their underwriting authority including the participation in file reviews, UQR Audits and Stewardship meetings. Participating in insurance & industry specific association training/meetings to enhance knowledge by class of business to stay current with industry specific market trends as it pertains to assigned Program(s). Collaborating with Actuarial, Accounting, Claims, Operations, Program Development, Risk Management and Underwriting as necessary. Overseeing the implementation of new business programs assigned to you including coordination with Operations, IT and other functional support areas. Maintaining current industry knowledge, market intelligence and competitor analysis through regular communication with assigned Program Administrator(s) and participation in external conferences, trade shows, etc."
    }
  ],

  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "#LI-JC1 At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Reserving & Analytics Actuary to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Make your mark in Actuarial Our Actuaries are on the front lines, quantifying risk, pricing insurance and reserving losses for AIG’s major insurance operating companies, globally. We collaborate with Finance, Claims, Operations, Marketing and Risk Management, among other functions. As an AIG actuary, you will be challenged and encouraged to reach your greatest potential to help improve how we predict, manage and mitigate risk. How you will create an impact As an actuary at AIG, you will be challenged and encouraged to reach your greatest potential. Every day will bring new opportunities to stretch your analytical and problem-solving skills as you improve how we predict and mitigate risk. Our Financial Actuaries are on the frontlines, quantifying risk, pricing insurance and reserving losses for AIG’s major regulated and rated insurance operating companies globally. Portfolio Analytics and Pricing Actuaries support AIG’s businesses and are responsible for technical pricing, product development analytics and performance measurement. They collaborate with Marketing, Operations and Financial Management. The successful candidate will be supporting the AIG APAC insurance business, and work as a member of the APAC actuarial team to achieve goals and deliver on strategic initiatives. The candidate is expected to work as part of the regional team to provide analytical insights to the business functions including profit centres, underwriting, finance, and risk management and also provide local regulatory support for the assigned APAC countries. Key responsibilities include: As a member of the APAC regional actuarial team, coordinate US GAAP quarterly management reporting requirements with the regional tower leads: Assist in supporting strategic portfolio reviews and deep dive analyses of key business segments within APAC; Preparation of local regulatory reporting for AIG’s Australia and New Zealand offices. This includes: Quarterly Board and Risk committee reporting; Quarterly solvency calculations and IFRS 17 disclosures Annual Insurance Liability Valuation Report and Financial Condition / Stress Testing Report; Review of capital projections and investment strategies including ALM (“Asset Liability Matching); Provide support to the budget loss ratio exercise and annual financial planning; Assist in other ad-hoc analyses as required. What you’ll need to succeed Extensive general insurance reserving experience, preferably in long tail lines. Proficiency in actuarial concepts/methodologies and ability to educate non-technical managers on technical areas that enhance their decision-making ability; Strong analytical and problem solving skills; Effective project & time management and strong communication skills; Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously; Ability to work as part of a team as well as take responsibility for his individual contribution Ability to meet tight deadlines and prioritise own work; Ability to forge relationships with other areas of the business; Working knowledge on typical actuarial reserving software; Professional behaviour with high level of integrity; Applicants must have valid rights to work in Australia, as employer sponsorship is not available for this position. Ready to make a bigger impact? We look forward to reviewing your application. At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. Welcome to a culture of inclusion We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com."
    }

      "content": "At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as an Underwriter I, Excess & Surplus Lines to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. The Production Specialist must be able to illustrate a high level of technical proficiency and underwriting acumen combined with marketing and sales skills to meet production underwriting goals while maintaining budgeted loss and expense ratios. Candidates are expected to work effectively in a team environment to develop broker and portfolio strategies in addition to working independently to manage a profitable book of new and renewal business. The Zone will rely heavily on this position to assist in guiding, mentoring and training more junior members of the team, as well as to assist in planning and budgeting, collaboration across AIG divisions and building a culture of diversity and inclusion across the team."
    },

      "section_title": "Qualifications and Skills",
      "content": "The candidate must have the ability to underwrite property risks across the spectrum of E&S Property business while helping to set direction for your team and maintaining a balanced portfolio across both primary and excess placements. An insurance professional with a minimum 3 years' experience in Commercial Property E&S underwriting. Ability to underwrite 100%, primary/shared and layered, and excess structures. Strong understanding of current market conditions, ability to quickly make decisions and turn around quotes and manage multiple prospects at one time. Must be able to work with regional Property leadership peers to build a nationally renowned team and culture for Lexington. Underwrite risks to maintain and profitably grow our E&S portfolio across desired business segments. Collaborate internally and with wholesale trading partners."
    },

  "company_name": "AIG Europe S.A.- General Representative for Italy",
  "job_link": "https://aig.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/job/Milano/Senior-Underwriter-Construction_JR2601243-1",

  "locations": ["Milano"],

  "required_skill_set": [
    "Proven underwriting experience",
    "Good knowledge of Construction",

    "Strong communication skills",

    "Organizational and time management skills",
    "Customer and Sales orientated behaviors",
    "Mentoring and training interest"
  ],
  "remote_type": "In-office",
  "posted_on": "Posted 9 Days Ago",
  "job_id": "JR2601243",
  "salary": "",
  "is_active": true,
  "first_seen": "2023-10-20",
  "last_seen": "2023-10-29",
  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "Senior Construction Underwriter Join us as a Senior Underwriter to make a greater impact through your technical expertise and people skills. This could be the ideal chance to step up into your Senior level role. Make your mark in Underwriting AIG underwriting teams help to find insurance solutions in areas including Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Specialty Lines, Cyber, Multinational Clients, and High Net Worth individuals. We are reimagining how we help customers to manage risk, transforming our operating model, and reshaping our role responsibilities and career pathways. The goal is to unlock the full potential in each colleague – empowering our people to grow as insurance professionals and add more value to our customers and AIG. How you will create an impact This role is your opportunity to drive profitable growth in Construction, by working on creative solutions for complex risks. You will be responsible for underwriting a range of new and renewal business, using your proven relationship building skills. We want you to work closely with brokers to communicate risk appetite and our value proposition – and to understand the deal structures, terms and products that best meets clients’ needs. You will then negotiate pricing, terms & conditions, and deal structure with brokers and clients. It will be important to generate new business by expanding your broker network, attending broker events, and building new relationships across the market. You will collaborate with AIG Distribution, territory managers, and underwriters in other business lines to target desirable accounts and cross-selling opportunities. As a senior member of the team, you will set a high standard of customer service to brokers and clients – providing an example for junior colleagues to follow. You will also provide guidance, mentoring and training to less-experienced underwriters – and support them in building their broker network. What you’ll need to succeed Proven underwriting experience, with good knowledge of Construction. The ability to analyze financial statements. Strong communication, networking and relationship building skills. Good organizational and time management skills. Customer and Sales orientated behaviors: motivated, tenacious, focused, technically skilled, proactive, and accountable. An interest in mentoring and training if you have not held these duties previously. Ready to step up to new challenges? We would love to hear from you. At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. We created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. Welcome to a culture of inclusion We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com."
    }
  ],

}

Get to know the business

Our Global Sourcing & Procurement Services (GS&PS) colleagues manage all AIG third party spend, ensuring maximized productivity and quality. Our global team includes Category Strategy, Negotiations and Execution, Transformation, Operations, Productivity, Environmental Sustainability, Supplier Diversity, Analytics, Tools, and Program Governance. GS&PS is committed to providing value to our stakeholders through building trust, providing a best-in-class procurement experience, maximizing profit, minimizing risk, and enabling sustainable growth for AIG.

About the role

We are looking for an experienced Strategic Sourcing Manager for Market Data, focused on developing and implementing strategies for acquiring financial market data and ensuring optimal cost efficiency, data quality, and vendor management. The ideal candidate will have a proven record in strategic sourcing within the financial services sector with exceptional negotiation skills, a strong understanding of market data dynamics, and the ability to collaborate effectively with various stakeholders to meet strategic goals.

Responsibilities:

  • Develop, implement, and manage comprehensive category strategies for financial market data, including subscriptions, real-time feeds, historical data, analytical platforms, and related services, to drive optimal cost, quality, and service levels across the enterprise.

  • Perform in-depth market analyses, identifying trends in financial data services, emerging data providers and technologies, opportunities for cost optimization, and potential supply chain risks.

  • Partner with cross-functional teams and key stakeholders to define market data requirements, ensure alignment with overall business objectives and regulatory mandates, and optimize data utilization.

  • Lead and execute complex negotiations for market data contracts and licenses with providers, optimizing terms, pricing models, and ensuring strict compliance with company policies, licensing agreements, and industry regulations.

  • Identify, develop, and implement continuous improvement initiatives for market data sourcing processes, enhancing efficiency, transparency, and achieving measurable cost savings and cost avoidance.

  • Develop strong and collaborative relationships with critical market data suppliers and internal stakeholders.

  • Develop, implement, and report on key performance indicators (KPIs) and service level agreements (SLAs) specific to market data; Track progress towards strategic goals, drive accountability for supplier performance, and maintain a strong governance framework for market data acquisition and usage.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Business Administration, Supply Chain Management, or a related field is required. An MBA or advanced degree is strongly preferred, particularly with a focus on quantitative analysis, financial markets, or technology management.

  • 7+ years of progressive experience in strategic sourcing, category management, or procurement, with a focus in the Market Data category within financial services or insurance and/or IT sourcing.

  • Deep understanding of the financial market data landscape, including different data types (real-time, historical, reference data), delivery mechanisms, licensing models, and key providers.

  • Strong understanding of financial market operations and how market data supports various business functions.

  • Excellent negotiation skills with a demonstrated track record of successfully negotiating complex market data contracts and licenses.

  • Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills to effectively engage with and influence senior business stakeholders, legal, compliance teams, and external market data providers.

  • Strong proficiency in managing contract lifecycles, ensuring compliance, and mitigating associated risks.

  • Strong understanding of relevant financial regulations and data privacy regulations impacting market data procurement and usage

  • Proficiency in market data inventory and usage management systems and procurement software (e.g., SAP Ariba).

#LI-AIG1

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike.

Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters

At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family.

Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become.

Welcome to a culture of inclusion

We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion.       "content": "FNOL Cabin Agent Who we are. American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. AIG member companies offer a wide range of casualty and casualty insurance in approximately 70 countries and jurisdictions. This diverse offering includes products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets and manage risk. We are also committed to making a positive difference for our colleagues and the communities where we work and live. We encourage our colleagues to contribute to the causes that matter most to them by supporting these initiatives through our Volunteer Time Off Programs and Matching Grants. Get to know the business General Insurance is a leading provider of insurance products and services for business and personal customers. It has one of the most extensive property insurance networks in the world. General Insurance offers a wide range of products to its customers through a diversified, multi-channel distribution network. The Customer Service team collaborates with the company to support policyholders and brokers on a wide range of products and offers, through each interaction during 24/7 business hours, 365 days a year. Each CC agent is responsible for delivering and materializing the product offer and promise. Our main objective is to provide support, experience and an excellent service in emergency care. The FNOL Cabin Agent has the responsibility to provide immediate attention to the requests of our policyholders, even in situations where health and safety are compromised. On paper The FNOL Cabin Agent is in charge of providing immediate and quality attention to our policyholders when they suffer a vehicle or home accident or require any type of assistance that we offer in our products. The position is part of the Mexico Contact Center team, responsible for assisting our customers during a claim or who need assistance with a 24/7 service schedule, in accordance with local regulations and the criteria established by AIG guidelines. In 2025, FNOL's Claims and Assistance Contact Center in Mexico answered approximately 200,000 calls. The main activities of this agent are: Register new claims in our systems due to traffic accidents, roadside assistance, etc. Carry out the correct validation of the status and collection of the policy. Customer service support, providing the necessary information to follow up on your claim. What you need to know: The contact center is not only essential to support the business team and attend to the insured in case of claim or assistance, but also to avoid financial and regulatory impacts. In 2025 it achieved KPIs above the market with 90% in Service Level and 3% in Abandoned calls. AUTO: Call option: 96,444 options / Portfolio value: USD 33 million in annualized premiums. Package: Call option: 10,320 options / Portfolio value: USD 5 million in annualized premiums. Collateral: Call option: 85,812 options / Portfolio value: USD 16 million in annualized premiums. Positional performance has a direct positive impact in recent years on insured NPS survey results from 53% in 2020 to 64% in 2025. Your contribution to AIG The FNOL Cabin Agent is tasked with gathering detailed information to provide the care of car accidents, personal accidents, business lines and multiple assistance. Through knowledge of the product and care processes, as well as the correct use of our main systems, to meet the needs of our policyholders at the moment of truth and avoid an economic or reputational impact."

  "educational_qualifications": "Bachelor’s or Advanced Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Risk Management, Business Administration, or related field",
  "required_skill_set": [
    "Audit experience in risk management activities",
    "Leadership in audit engagements",
    "Knowledge of risk disciplines including model, third party, operational risk",
    "Strong communication and project management skills",
    "Experience with data analysis tools (e.g., MS Excel, Access, ACL, Tableau)",
    "Professional certifications (CPA, FRM, CRM, CIA) preferred",
    "Experience in financial services/insurance industries a plus",
    "Familiarity with insurance laws and regulations",
    "Ability to work independently and in a team",
    "Strong leadership and relationship-building skills"
  ],
  "remote_type": "On-site primarily, with some travel",
  "posted_on": "Posted 15 Days Ago",
  "job_id": "JR2601194",
  "salary": "$94,000-$118,000 plus bonus",
  "is_active": true,
  "first_seen": "2024-04-10",
  "last_seen": "2024-04-25",
  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Responsibilities",
      "content": "Ensure key risks and controls are properly identified, test procedures support the audit objectives, test procedures are completed in the budgeted timeframe and work papers support the work performed and conclusions drawn in accordance with IAG’s Global Audit Methodology and professional standards for audit engagements. Perform data analysis to identify anomalies or issues in the testing of controls, partnering with the IAG data analytics team. Draft identified issues, audit reports, process flows and narratives, and other documents to assist audit team with reporting and effectively discuss findings with business stakeholders. Work closely with the audit engagement teams to verify management’s corrective actions for audit issues within specified time frames by testing control design and operating effectiveness. Hold team accountable for meeting deliverables, quality assurance and methodology standards, including providing real-time feedback. Identify opportunities to enhance operational efficiency and effectiveness for areas under audit. Build and maintain strong working relationships internally as well as with the business. Provide guidance and direction for junior staff to develop business and audit knowledge, set clear expectations, provide timely and constructive feedback, encourage their career advancement, and set the tone by enthusiastically facilitating knowledge exchange/transfer. Ensure audit projects are delivered on time and project staff is utilized efficiently. Some travel may be required."
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Qualifications",
      "content": "6+ years of audit experience in risk management activities at large financial institutions with a focus on model, third party and operational risk disciplines and/or audit experience in 2 or more of the above mentioned risk disciplines. At least 3 years of experience leading audit engagements performing in the role of Audit Manager. Bachelor’s or Advanced Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Risk Management, Business Administration, or related field. At least one certified professional designation (e.g., CPA, FRM, CRM, CIA) is preferred. Experience working in the financial services/insurance industries a plus. Familiarity with Insurance industry applicable laws and regulations. Managerial courage to effectively deliver difficult messages to staff members and determine appropriate reporting and escalation as needed. Ability to thrive within a team-oriented environment while focusing on what matters most. Good at cultivating relationships with stakeholders and within the IAG group, building trust that facilitates an open mindset and free exchange of information. Function independently and multi-task in a dynamic, changing environment. Strong leadership skills; attract and develop top talent. Strong communication (oral and written), presentation, and project management skills. Possess and promote a culture of intellectual curiosity. Experience with auditing leveraging the use of data analysis tools is a plus (e.g., MS Excel tools, Access, ACL, Tableau etc.)."
    }
  ],

  "salary": "",

  "first_seen": "2023-10-09",
  "last_seen": "2023-10-09",
  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "Associate Underwriter M&A (w/m/d) Standort: Frankfurt a.M. / München Seit über 100 Jahren stehen wir weltweit für unsere Expertise zur Absicherung komplexer Risiken. In diesem partnerschaftlichen Sinne unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, einer ungewissen Zukunft vertrauensvoll entgegenzublicken. In Deutschland ist die AIG seit 70 Jahren aktiv und steht den Versicherten seitdem mit lokalen Teams versierter Experten an vier Standorten jederzeit zur Seite. Bei uns werden Sie nicht nur Teil eines ambitionierten Teams bei einem der weltweit führenden Versicherungsunternehmen, sondern haben gleichzeitig die Möglichkeit, an der Gestaltung nationaler und internationaler Risikotransfers hautnah beteiligt zu sein. Für die Position des Associate Underwriter M&A (w/m/d) suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen neuen Kollegen (m/w/d) zur Verstärkung unseres Teams am Standort Frankfurt oder München. Was Sie erwartet: Koordination des Underwriting-Prozesses von der Anfrage bis zum Abschluss der Police Unterstützung bei der Risikobewertung von M&A-Transaktionen im Hinblick auf die Gewährleistungen des Verkäufers Vorbereitung von Standardverträgen (Indikationen, Vertraulichkeits- und Beitrittsvereinbarungen, Expense Agreements, Policen) Teilnahme an und Nachbearbeitung von Underwriting Calls Erstellen und Unterstützung des Verhandelns der Police mit Kunden und deren Anwälten Eigenverantwortliche Übernahme von begleitenden administrativen und organisatorischen Aufgaben in Zusammenhang mit dem Underwriting-Prozess unter der Erstellung und Buchung von Policen Unterstützung bei der Koordination und Korrespondenz mit Maklern und externen Rechtsberatern sowie der Abstimmung mit ausländischen Kollegen Pflege von Beziehungen zu Private Equity Unternehmen, Inhouse M&A Abteilungen, Investment Banking Teams, spezialisierten Versicherungsmaklern und Anwaltskanzleien Unterstützung des Head of M&A Northern Europe und der Schadensabteilung bei operativen Aufgaben wie z.B. Erstellung und fortlaufende Auswertung von Statistiken Unterstützung des Head of M&A Northern Europe sowie des Head of M&A Europe bei der Auswertung von Statistiken in Bezug auf Schäden, Prämienentwicklung und Salesforce Kennzahlen. Was Sie mitbringen: Abgeschlossenes Studium der Rechts-, Ingenieur- oder Wirtschaftswissenschaften, Masterabschluss einer Universität oder Business School (MBA, LL.M., etc.) wünschenswert Mindestens erste Berufserfahrung im Bereich M&A Erste relevante Berufserfahrung im Versicherungswesen bzw. im Banking Exzellente Kommunikationsfähigkeiten sowie verhandlungssicheres Deutsch und Englisch (weitere Fremdsprachen von Vorteil) Teamfähigkeit und schnelle Auffassungsgabe sowie Entscheidungsfreude Hohe Belastbarkeit besonders bei der Arbeit unter Stress und Termindruck sowie hohes Durchsetzungsvermögen Gute Word-Kenntnisse, Grundkenntnisse bzgl. Excel- und PowerPoint Bereitschaft zur Reisetätigkeit Warum wir die beste Wahl sind: Wir bieten Ihnen vielfältige Aufgaben in einem digitalen und internationalen Umfeld mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten. Wir arbeiten in flachen Hierarchien mit echten Gestaltungsmöglichkeiten. Teamarbeit steht bei uns an oberster Stelle. Regelmäßige Diversity & Inclusion-Veranstaltungen sowie Corporate Benefits und Fahrradleasing runden unsere Arbeitswelt ab. Zusatzleistungen wie 30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr, arbeitsfreie Tage am 24.12. und 31.12., betriebliche Altersvorsorge und vermögenswirksame Leistungen sind für uns selbstverständlich. Wir haben Ihr Interesse geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihren Lebenslauf direkt über unser Bewerbungsportal. Bei Fragen erreichen Sie Hanna Gamper (Talent Acquisition) unter +49 (0) 69 97113 234. #LI-AIG At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matter."
    }
  ],

  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "Join us as an Underwriting Specialist, National Accounts, Foreign Casualty to step up to a key team role that will drive business growth. Make your mark in Underwriting. We are reimagining how we help customers to manage risk, transforming our operating model, and reshaping our role responsibilities and career pathways. The goal is to unlock the full potential in each colleague – empowering our people to grow as insurance professionals and add more value to our customers and AIG. This is a key role in our Foreign Casualty team in which you will take ownership for achieving goals and outcomes. You will be tasked with overseeing a range of programs and processes, in addition to handling complex underwriting. You will thrive by successfully balancing the needs of multiple stakeholders and making sound decisions using data, analysis, experience, judgment, and a risk mindset. It will be essential to build effective relationships with brokers and to deliver a high standard of customer service as an example for the team to follow. You will promote a culture of collaboration, inclusivity, and learning while mentoring and developing colleagues through role modeling and training programs. You will aim to facilitate personal growth and career development, supporting succession planning. You will also play a key role in new business development, including attending broker events and building new relationships across the market."
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Benefits",
      "content": "Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters. At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family."
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Company Culture",
      "content": "Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world. American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become."
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Inclusion & Equal Opportunity",
      "content": "We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com."
    }

  "id": "JR2600637",
  "title": "Financial Lines Underwriter, Italy",
  "company_name": "AIG Europe S.A.- General Representative for Italy",
  "job_link": "https://aig.wd1.myworkdayjobs.com/job/Milano/Junior-Financial-Lines-Underwriter--Italy_JR2600637-1",
  "experience": "Some underwriting experience is preferred; however, other professional experience is acceptable.",
  "locations": ["Milano"],
  "educational_qualifications": "",
  "required_skill_set": [
    "Ability to analyze financial statements",
    "Strong communication skills",
    "Networking and relationship building skills",
    "Good organizational and time management skills",
    "Customer and Sales oriented behaviors: motivated, tenacious, focused, technically skilled, proactive, and accountable"
  ],
  "remote_type": "In-office",
  "posted_on": "2023-07-04",
  "job_id": "JR2600637",
  "salary": "",
  "is_active": true,
  "first_seen": "2023-07-04",
  "last_seen": "2023-07-04",
  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "Financial Lines Underwriter At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a underwriter to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. Make your mark in Underwriting AIG underwriting teams help to find insurance solutions in areas including Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Specialty Lines, Cyber, Multinational Clients, and High Net Worth individuals. We are reimagining how we help customers to manage risk, transforming our operating model, and reshaping our role responsibilities and career pathways. The goal is to unlock the full potential in each colleague – empowering our people to grow as insurance professionals and add more value to our customers and AIG. This role will focus on underwriting new and renewal business to help drive the profitable growth of our Financial Lines, Financial institutions. You will be responsible for assessing the desirability of risk through regular contact with brokers, close co-ordination with AIG subject matter experts and robust reviews of claims history and general company information. Your relationships with brokers will be key to your success. You will need to provide excellent service to brokers through a consistent, timely, and proactive approach to underwriting. You will also aim to generate new business by expanding your broker network, attending broker events, and building new relationships across the market – in line with a sales plan established with your manager. Another key aspect of the role is the provision of accurate monthly management information including submissions, bind rate, average premium, and total premium. At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become.       "content": "#LI-JC1 At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. We are seeking an experienced Casualty Underwriter to join our Major Accounts team, underwriting large corporate accounts across a diverse range of industries, including construction, energy and mining. You will work on some of the largest organisations operating in Australia and globally, partnering with major brokerages to structure liability programmed for complex business. This role focusses on large corporate risks across the general business including mining, energy and construction sectors, working with major multinational clients and leading brokerages. These organisations represent some of the largest industrial businesses operating in Australia and internationally. This is a senior technical underwriting role, requiring strong experience in complex casualty placements, broker engagement and portfolio management. Make your mark in Casualty Underwriting AIG Underwriting teams help to find insurance solutions in areas including Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Specialty Lines, Cyber, and Multinational Clients. We are reimagining how we help customers to manage risk, transforming our operating model and reshaping our role responsibilities and career pathways. The goal is to unlock the full potential in each colleague – empowering our people to grow as insurance professionals and add more value to our customers and AIG. How you will create an impact As a Senior Underwriter at AIG, you will assess and underwrite risks for large corporate clients, structure liability programs and work closely with brokers and internal stakeholder to deliver solutions for complex organisations. You will contribute to the continued growth of our Casualty portfolio, underwriting businesses across a broad range of industries including infrastructure, construction, mining, energy and manufacturing. This role provides exposure to large and multinational insured’s requiring strong technical underwriting capability and commercial judgement. As a Senior Casualty Underwriter your key accountabilities include, but not limited to: Underwrite large corporate casualty accounts, including complex liability programs Partner with key brokerages to structure insurance solutions for high-revenue corporate clients and multinational organisations. Analyse complex operational exposures and apply sound underwriting judgment when assessing risk and pricing. Attend client and broker meetings to develop a strong understanding of risk management frameworks and operational exposures, presenting tailored insurance solutions. Manage and retain an existing portfolio while contributing to growth of new corporate business, maintaining strong underwriting discipline within delegated authority. Achieve agreed production targets, executing strategic marketing initiatives and strengthening broker relationships. Increase AIG market share within the Casualty segment through the strengthening of relationships with key brokers, clients and industry groups. Contribute to strategies to grow profitable business within target sectors, working with other profit centres to identify cross selling opportunities. Monitor and report on portfolio performance including production, retention, new business, loss ratio, claims activity. Collaborate with internal stakeholders including actuarial, legal, claims, finance and operations to support underwriting standards and portfolio performance. Maintain open communication with Segment Managers and other underwriting leaders regarding market developments and portfolio activity. What you’ll need to succeed Casualty Underwriting experience in the corporate or large commercial segment across a broad range of products including Primary Casualty, Excess/Umbrella Experience in complex liability risks or larger revenue organisations Strong understanding of Primary Casualty, Excess and Umbrella structures Ability to engage confidently with senior broker partners and corporate"

  ],
  "remote_type": "In-office",

  "salary": "",
  "is_active": true,
  "first_seen": "2023-09-10",
  "last_seen": "2023-09-10",
  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Purpose",
      "content": "損害保険ビジネスにおいて、アンダーライティング（保険引受業務）は中核かつ不可欠なものであり、アンダーライティング部門が行う意思決定は、保険会社の損益に直接影響します。延長保証保険のビジネスはAIGがグローバルで強みをもつ分野であり、今後の日本市場でのさらなる成長に向けて、アンダーライティング部門は延長保証保険の制度設計、販売促進、代理店教育、収支管理などへの貢献が期待されます。"
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Job Responsibilities",
      "content": "延長保証のアンダーライティング部門のマネージャーとして、関連部門との連携の元、延長保証制度設計において、制度構築、引受判断、保険料設定等をリードして頂き、Head of Extended Warrantyへのレポートを行います。また、案件によってはGlobalのExtended Warrantyチームとの連携も必要となります。ブローカー、代理店等と協力しての販売促進をリードして頂きます。また、複雑な案件の場合は、顧客企業への直接的な説明を行う場合もあります。通常の商品管理業務（約款解釈の照会対応、募集文書チェック）およびプロジェクトとしての商品開発業務（約款改定、料率改定等）も職責の一つとなります。"
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Key Relationships",
      "content": "内部関係先 (AIG組織内): AIG Globalの延長保証保険ライン（海外）、その他の商品ライン（主に国内）、営業部門、損害サービス部門、AIGグループにおける保証ビジネス管理会社。外部関係先 (AIG組織外): ディストリビューション・パートナー（代理店／ブローカーなど）。"
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Required Skills and Experiences",
      "content": "最終学歴: 学士号または同等の学位。スキル/能力: 論理的思考力、調整能力およびコミュニケーション力、正確な日本語を読み書きできる能力、英語での業務に抵抗がない程度の語学力。業務経験: 損害保険業界における豊富な業務経験（5年以上望ましい）、新しいチャレンジと仕組みを作りに積極的に取り組める方、困難に直面しても粘り強く前向きに解決策を考えることができる方。尚可: 損害保険会社の商品業務部門における実務経験、損害保険の商品開発実務経験。"
    }
  ],

}

  "company_name": "AIG Technologies (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd",

  "experience": "15+ years of full-time related work experience in information security management and/or related functions",
  "locations": ["Kuala Lumpur"],
  "educational_qualifications": "Not specified",
  "required_skill_set": [
    "Leadership in information security",
    "Risk management",
    "Cybersecurity",
    "Regulatory compliance",
    "Security architecture",
    "Communication skills",
    "Technical IT background",
    "Security metrics and KPIs",
    "Experience in financial or insurance industry",
    "Security certifications such as SANS, ISC2, ISACA, ISO 27001"
  ],
  "remote_type": "On-site",
  "posted_on": "2023-10-02",
  "job_id": "JR2600953",
  "salary": "Not specified",
  "is_active": true,
  "first_seen": "2023-09-03",
  "last_seen": "2023-10-02",
  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "Responsible for providing security leadership, advice and counsel to executive management teams on security policy and practices; identifies exposures and threats and drives corrective plans based on risk. Implement the Company’s information security strategy across the business environments. Ensure the enterprise information security architecture and roadmaps are implemented and meet both business and information security objectives. Identify and understand key business processes, systems and specific security needs critical to Business Unit, and ensure they are incorporated into the overall cybersecurity strategy. Help develop business cases, secure leadership sponsorship, and drive adoption and implementation of security projects and programs. Support the business in its compliance to Malaysia Risk Management in Technology (RMiT) or any other relevant regulatory guidelines/standards issued by the local regulator. Help formulate and facilitate the effective implementation of relevant technology risk/information security standards required by the respective regulators (Eg RMiT Technology Risk Management Framework, and Cyber Resilience Framework) for the local entity. Ensure security projects and activities are prioritized based on risk to the business unit; work with leadership team to develop risk management program to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information owned, controlled or processed by the Company. Support anticipating, identifying, and deterring cyber-based attacks against AIG and its business operations, employees, and subsidiaries. Provide support to central team developing threat response and risk mitigation plans, including input into scenarios that involve invoking countermeasures and containment strategies. Ensure security issues are addressed with timely, appropriate responses to minimize the impact to the Company, or its assets, customers or reputation. Monitor and evaluate risk performance metrics on key security issues and programs, recommend corrective action programs as appropriate, and drive remediation items to completion. Actively contribute to the matters being discussed at the management committees (e.g., risk committee meetings, Board of Directors meeting) where information security is an invitee or a member, and where relevant, provide inputs of the matters under the information security purview. Remain current on constantly emerging Cybersecurity and geopolitical threats to ensure continual protection of the Company’s assets, information and reputation, ensuring the risk balance between risk posture and business agility."
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Requirements",
      "content": "Prior experience leading mid-size information security teams with a strong track record of success in complex environments. 15+ years of full-time related work experience in information security management and/or related functions (such as Technology Risk Management). At least 3 years holding a senior security leadership position at a company. Ability to communicate information security risks effectively in business terms to all levels of the organization. Experience in financial or insurance industry preferred, with an in-depth understanding of the relevant local regulatory requirements. Information security related certification such as SANS, ISC2, ISACA, ISO 27001 will be desirable. Trustworthy, proactive and takes leadership, with high standards of personal integrity. Typically, also possesses a background in technical IT roles such as security architecture, technology risk and governance or cyber security strategy - with an overarching information security oversight. Knowledge of security metrics and Key Security Risk indicators and how to implement them. Ability to work locally without need for visa/work permit sponsorship."
    }
  ],

    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "#LI- AIG Start your career as Property Analyst Location: United Arab Emirates, Dubai AIG offers its customers a wide range of tailored insurance solutions and is one of the largest international insurance companies in the world. We are 36,000+ colleagues providing services in approximately 70 countries and jurisdictions. AIG’s Underwriters embody our tradition of innovation, by bringing to market ground-breaking insurance solutions for our changing world. AIG’s underwriters help to find insurance solutions for risks from a range of areas including Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Specialty Lines, Cyber, Multinational Clients and High Net Worth individuals. Your start @ AIG Our Early Career Program is aimed at graduates from all disciplines who are looking to integrate a diverse international community and to start a career that nurtures their potential. The Early Career Program is for those who enjoy exchanges and negotiations and who could see themselves interacting with brokers and customers internationally. The goal of our program is to help you build an exciting and fulfilling career at AIG. You will have the chance to join one of our key lines: Financial Lines, Property, Casualty, Multinational, Package or Claims. In addition to the training on-the-job, you will participate in the AIG Academy, a two-year structured development program designed to support and accelerate your strengths and interests. To help you succeed, we make sure you receive above-average remuneration, regardless of your degree. You will be accompanied by a mentor, and you will have the chance to participate in global workshops, master classes, as well as networking events to get to know colleagues from the AIG worldwide. You will receive a permanent employment contract. What you can expect: Support and independent review of risks Preparation of customized offers for national and international customers High-level of personal responsibility and entrepreneurial work in a helpful and committed team English-speaking communication with local and foreign brokers, customers, and colleagues of the company's own international network Collaboration on interdepartmental and strategic projects Steep learning curve and a variety of development opportunities What you bring: Bachelor's or Master's degree, e.g. in Business Administration, Economics, Information Technology, Law, Engineering, Actuarial Sciences or Risk Management, graduated in 2025 or 2026. Initial work experience through internships is an advantage, ideally in the insurance sector Motivated by innovation and novelty Readiness to be on a steep learning curve and agile comprehension skills Ability to work in a team, as well as willingness to make decisions Very good English language skills MS Office skills We’re proud to offer a range of employee benefits and resources that help you protect what matters most – your health care, savings, financial protection, and life quality. Our global mental health and wellness days off provide all colleagues with an opportunity to focus on their wellbeing. We offer you diverse, exciting tasks in a digital and international environment with flexible working hours and smart working. We work in flat hierarchies with real opportunities for creativity. Teamwork is our top priority. Interested? Become a part of our team! Start dates: July 01st, 2026 What you’ll need to know: At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why team members come together in the office four days per week. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Diversity and inclusion are central components of our corporate culture and crucial to the success of our company today and in the future. They form the foundation, give room to creativity, make us more innovative, and contribute to growth and profitability. We promote the development of our employees with numerous programs and initiatives. After all, our employees should not only be respected as individuals, but also valued for their unique perspectives. Learn more about AIG's values here."
    }

      "content": "AIG ist eines der größten internationalen Versicherungsunternehmen und bietet seinen Kund:innen eine große Bandbreite verschiedener maßgeschneiderter Versicherungslösungen in rund 70 Ländern und Jurisdiktionen an.       "section_title": "Program Details",

Effective communication of key Complex claims and (LoB) portfolio messages to internal stakeholders. Financial control through consistent reserving and other financial transaction discipline. We position our colleagues to reach their full potential by providing them with the tools and opportunities to develop their career while maintaining a work/life balance. With a focus on Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) efforts, one of AIG and HR’s highest priorities, HR fosters a sense of belonging and encourages our colleagues to be themselves in the workplace. Provide support to the talent acquisition team for early career recruitment, implement best practices in sourcing and candidate engagement. Execute and coordinate HR initiatives, employee engagement programs to foster an inclusion organizational culture. Organize and host new hires' orientation.   "remote_type": "On-site",

  "job_id": "Underwriter--Cyber_JR2600819",
  "salary": "",
  "is_active": true,
  "first_seen": "2026-02-23",
  "last_seen": "2026-02-23",
  "additional_sections": [
    {
      "section_title": "Job Description",
      "content": "AIG is a market leader in Cyber Insurance. Our Cyber portfolio has experienced significant growth in the last 24 months in terms of both GWP and policy count. We see the continuation of that growth as a key long-term business objective and are seeking the right individual to play a critical role in contributing to both the retention and growth of our UK Cyber and Tech PI portfolio of business. The role will be supported by the Financial Lines underwriting team including close and regular contact with our cyber colleagues based in London. Regular training on technical developments, current trends and claims insights will be provided across our international cyber team. You will use your existing knowledge along with the training provided to contribute to the cyber and Tech PI underwriting strategy across our portfolio. This will range from detailed underwriting of large UK based clients to volume based digital solutions for SME clients. This role can be based in any of our regional offices in Manchester, Glasgow or Birmingham."
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Impact and Responsibilities",
      "content": "Meet individual underwriting expectations to help team achieve budgeted financial objectives. Underwrite within agreed decision-making standards, limitations, and underwriting authority. Assess desirability of risk through analysis of financial reports and accounts; regular contact with broker; thorough review of proposal; and review of claims history and general company information. Use designated Underwriting system(s) to streamline underwriting and information-tracking approach across Profit Centre. Input required data consistently and accurately. Provide Management information including, but not limited to, submissions, bind rate, average premium, and total premium as required. Provide excellent service to brokers through consistent, timely, and proactive approach to underwriting. Exhibit sales orientated behaviors, defined by Management as: motivated, tenacious, focused, technically skilled, proactive, and accountable. Generate new business by building on existing relationships and expanding broker network, attending broker events, market meetings, client 1 to 1’s and building new relationships across the market. Support and contribute to the Cyber & tech PI underwriting strategy within the UK regions portfolio. Maintain key renewal accounts at greater than 85% retention. Ensure all brokers and clients are treated fairly. Ensure contract certainty. Conduct self-audit as required. Ensure compliance with key regulatory and risk management responsibilities."
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Qualifications and Skills",
      "content": "Underwriting experience is preferred. The ability to analyze financial statements. Strong communication, networking and relationship building skills. Good organizational and time management skills. Customer-centric approach. Customer and Sales orientated behaviors: motivated, tenacious, focused, technically skilled, proactive, and accountable."
    },
    {
      "section_title": "Company Culture and Benefits",
      "content": "At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters. At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world. American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. Welcome to a culture of inclusion. We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories. AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com."
    }
